title: On Flaxman�s Penelope.
content:
The suitors sinn�d, but with a fair excuse,
    Whom all this elegance might well seduce;
    Nor can our censure on the husband fall,
    Who, for a wife so lovely, slew them all.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Cowper, titled "On Flaxman�s Penelope.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...
